tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible overtreatment of diabetes or poor nutritional intake. Elevated creatinine may indicate impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, and abnormal appearance, raising concern for renal pathology or dehydration. Hematocrit and platelet count are elevated, possibly reflecting hemoconcentration. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, review of diabetes management, and further evaluation for underlying renal or urinary tract disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
